own_review.issue: System.exit(-1) inside the catch(FluoException) of scanFluo sits inside the outer try-with-resources for FluoClient; exit() does not unwind the stack, so FluoClient.close() (zookeeper/connection cleanup) is skipped. The inner Snapshot is closed before the catch runs, but the client is not. A cleaner pattern is to set an exit flag / rethrow and exit in the CLI caller after resources close.

System.exit(-1) added to a shared core-module utility used by both FluoScan.main() and the deprecated AppRunner.scan(); both current callers are CLI-terminal so it is safe today and consistent with the existing exit(-1) at lines 106-107/158-159, but it makes the methods hostile to embedding/testing.
Leftover stray blank lines where the println/entriesFound statements were removed (empty first line of both methods, blank line replacing entriesFound++).

Moving 'Scan failed' to stderr and adding a non-zero exit code is the real bug fix here: previously a failed scan printed to stdout and the process exited 0, so scripts could not detect failure. Matches conventions in FluoWorkerImpl/FluoOracleImpl/BaseOpts.

Removal of 'Scanning snapshot...', 'No data found', and the entriesFound counter is the intended behavior of issue #981 (stop polluting stdout so scan output is pipeable/parseable) - not a defect. Claims that this contradicts the PR title misread the title.
License-header trailing-whitespace churn unrelated to the change; harmless.
Existing ScanTest only covers option parsing; no test asserts the new exit/stderr behavior or the cleaned-up output. Hard to test due to System.exit, which reinforces the design concern.

points.point: PR title 'contradicts the natural reading' / ambiguous intent (ScanUtil.java:90-93)
points.why: Issue #981 asked exactly for stopping the meta-info printing; the title 'stopped printing meta info in scan' matches the diff. The contradiction claim is false and the requested clarification is noise.

points.point: stderr switch is correct, consistent with FluoWorkerImpl 87/94, FluoOracleImpl 90/97, ScanUtil 106/158
points.why: All line citations verified in repo; matches the human point's underlying bug (errors invisible to scripts).

points.point: System.exit(-1) consistent with existing pattern; both callers verified to have no code after scan; no library consumers
points.why: Verified: FluoScan.main and AppRunner.scan end after the call; only those two callers exist. Covers the human point. However 'the exit is safe' overlooks skipped FluoClient.close() (counted under missed).

points.point: Same err/exit change in scanAccumulo is good
points.why: Redundant restatement of the previous point for the second method.

points.point: Removing entriesFound/'No data found' leaves no way to distinguish empty result from silent failure
points.why: Hedged UX opinion that runs against the stated goal of #981; also overstated - after this PR failure exits 255 with stderr output, so the cases are distinguishable (only the checkError break path is silent).

points.point: Removing 'Scanning snapshot...' startup feedback is questionable
points.why: That removal is the core purpose of #981; hedged suggestion, no defect.

missed: System.exit(-1) inside the nested try-with-resources skips FluoClient.close() in scanFluo - v5 even asserted 'the exit is safe' (judge + prior-round nuance)
exceeds: Cross-file convention verification (FluoWorkerImpl/FluoOracleImpl line-accurate citations) and caller-safety analysis beyond the human reference

points.point: 'No data found' removal degrades UX; checkError break makes empty vs silently-broken output indistinguishable
points.why: Hedged UX concern against the stated goal of #981; the checkError silent-break observation is accurate but pre-existing behavior, and failure now carries exit 255.

points.point: Informational startup messages removal may be intentional; document rationale
points.why: This is the purpose of #981; hedged, no defect.

points.point: stderr + exit(-1) is a positive change matching ScanUtil 105-107/158-160 and FluoExec/FluoInit/BaseOpts; both callers verified, AppRunner noted as deprecated
points.why: All citations verified (FluoExec:49-50, FluoInit:125-126, BaseOpts:33-35); AppRunner @Deprecated at line 45 confirmed. Covers the human point's bug rationale.

points.point: exit(-1) becomes 255 on Unix; minor inconsistency notes vs FluoWait
points.why: Factually accurate (FluoWait uses exit(-1)) but low-actionability style note; consistent with the file's existing convention.

points.point: License-header whitespace churn could be a separate commit

missed: System.exit(-1) inside the nested try-with-resources skips FluoClient.close() in scanFluo (judge + prior-round nuance)
exceeds: Noted AppRunner is @Deprecated (verified) and the -1 -> 255 exit-code detail; cross-command convention check (FluoExec/FluoInit/BaseOpts)

qualitative: Both candidates correctly identified and verified the one substantive fix the human highlighted - moving scan failures to stderr with a non-zero exit code - with accurate cross-file citations. v5 undermines itself with a fabricated 'title contradicts the diff' claim (issue #981 asked for exactly this removal) and pads with redundant and intent-questioning points; v7 avoids any false claims and adds genuinely verified context (AppRunner deprecation, exit-code 255 semantics) but its top two points still second-guess the PR's stated purpose. Neither caught the real nuance that System.exit(-1) inside the nested try-with-resources skips FluoClient.close(); v5 explicitly (and wrongly in spirit) declared the exit 'safe'. Net scores tie at 0, but v7's review is cleaner: zero wrong points versus v5's one fabrication.
